changes_made: Renamed k8s_ingress_tls_enabled to k8s_ingress_tls_enforced for clarity
review_notes: The functions are generally well-mapped to Kubernetes security controls relevant to data leakage prevention. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but a minor change was made to improve clarity and consistency. The function k8s_ingress_tls_enabled was renamed to k8s_ingress_tls_enforced to better indicate the action being checked.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the action being enforced or checked, such as using 'enforced' instead of 'enabled' for TLS.
title: Data Leakage Prevention
step1_reasoning: The ISO27001 A.8.12 control focuses on preventing data leakage, which can be mapped to several Kubernetes security domains. Network policies can be used to restrict egress traffic, preventing unauthorized data exfiltration. Ensuring secrets and etcd are encrypted at rest protects sensitive data within the cluster. Enabling TLS for ingress ensures data in transit is encrypted, preventing interception.
